text: Whilst education inclusion policies have not always and do not always achieve their planned effects and sometimes have the contrary, as well as detrimental, effect for some pupils and social groups involved, such unintended or counter or exclusion effects are sometimes greater where policies and understandings are politically driven or framed. In any case the adverse potential for policies directed towards social integration and aimed at increasing the social participation of marginalised groups are inherent. A timely effort for a profound review of the educational inclusion policy that has been applied in an ever increasing number of countries, including countries of the European Union, bearing in mind that there are programmes already carried out that achieve results and in some way, at least to some extent, have been established as educational strategies in Catalan educational institutions as well as in schools and educational and vocational training centres in other Spanish regions, is therefore needed. Taking into account that the information included in this chapter is based on an on-going thematic literature review on national and international policy and the impacts of educational inclusion policy, past policies are considered together with some current official policies that are being implemented as well as an analysis of responsibilities associated to the implementation of inclusive education programme development in Spain.
headline: Fully Human Written
